heading: Considerations for Paused Clusters
body:
- You can't:

  - Change the configuration of a paused cluster.

  - Read data from or write data to a paused cluster.

  - Add or remove Search Nodes to a paused cluster.

- For paused clusters, Atlas:

  - Stops triggering configured alerts.

  - Stops all backups. Your existing snapshots remain until they expire.

  - Stops all [$search](pipe.$search) queries.

    > **Note:** When you pause a cluster, Atlas deletes all data from
    > the Search Nodes. Atlas creates new search nodes and
    > automatically rebuilds the MongoDB Search indexes when you resume the
    > Atlas cluster. While Atlas rebuilds the MongoDB Search indexes,
    > the [$search](pipe.$search) and [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) pipeline
    > stages are unavailable. When you resume the cluster, Atlas
    > only has metrics from the new search nodes as Atlas doesn't
    > retain any MongoDB Search metrics from before you paused the cluster.

- If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), when
  you resume a cluster, Atlas automatically enables
  Cloud Backup. If the Backup Compliance Policy has the
  Require Point in Time Restore to all clusters option
  set to On, Atlas automatically enables
  Continuous Cloud Backup and adjusts the restore window according to the
  Backup Compliance Policy. Atlas automatically modifies the backup to meet the
  minimum requirements of the Backup Compliance Policy.

  If you deployed Search Nodes separately, Atlas rebuilds the MongoDB Search
  indexes to restore the data on the Search Nodes that it deleted when you
  paused the Atlas cluster.

- If a paused cluster doesn't have [Encryption at Rest using Customer Key Management](security-kms-encryption) enabled, you can't toggle the
  **Require Encryption at Rest using Customer Key Management for all clusters** option to **On** in a Backup Compliance Policy.

- In certain scenarios, pausing a cluster pauses its required maintenance tasks.
  When you unpause the cluster, these maintenance tasks may start immediately, which
  could delay cluster availability.
